meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 9)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Review test: choose, identify, and correct pronoun usage.

questions.question: Which pronoun replaces 'the park' (a place)?
questions.explanation: "it" is used to replace a place or thing.

questions.question: "I" and "me" both mean 'myself', but they are used in different positions.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'I' is the subject (before verb). 'me' is the object (after verb).

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: "I am" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'am'.

questions.question: "He" is used for female people.
questions.explanation: "He" is for males. "She" is used for females.

questions.question: Which pronoun includes the speaker and others?
questions.explanation: "we" = the speaker + other people.

questions.question: Mina goes on a trip. → ___ goes on a trip. (one word)
questions.explanation: Mina is female → use 'She'.

questions.question: My dad always cooks dinner for me. → ___ always cooks dinner for me.
questions.explanation: My dad is male → use 'He'.

questions.question: Sort these: does the blank need 'I' or 'me'?
questions.explanation: 'I' = subject (before verb). 'me' = object (after verb).

questions.question: In the sentence "I have a class every Monday," the pronoun I is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'I' is the subject before the verb 'have'. This is correct.

questions.question: Fill in: My mom calls ___ every day. (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'me' is the object — it comes after the verb. My mom calls me.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "We go to the library." 'We' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' or 'me' correctly?
options: Can you give me the pencil?
questions.explanation: "give me" — 'me' is correctly used as the object after the verb 'give'.

questions.question: The sentence "Rosa and I paint it" is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Rosa and I' is the subject (before the verb). 'it' replaces an object.

questions.question: The grass is tall but it is soft. What does 'it' replace?
questions.explanation: "it" replaces "the grass" — the thing that is tall but soft.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ love dogs and cats. (I or me)
questions.hint: This is the subject of the sentence
questions.explanation: 'I' is the subject — it goes before the verb. I love dogs and cats.

questions.question: Match each noun phrase with the correct replacement pronoun:
questions.explanation: She = female. They = plural. He = male. It = thing/animal.

questions.question: Which sentence is CORRECT?
options: Me am not scared of the lion.
I am not scared of the lion.
My am not scared of the lion.
Mine am not scared of the lion.
questions.explanation: "I am" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'am'. This is correct.

questions.question: The sentence "A lion sees I" contains a grammar error.
questions.explanation: After the verb 'sees', we need the object pronoun 'me': "A lion sees me."

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "She always helps me with homework." 'me' is the object after 'helps'.

questions.question: Which sentence has a WRONG pronoun?
options: My mom calls me every day.
questions.explanation: "Me love" is wrong — 'Me' cannot be the subject. Correct: "I love dogs and cats."
